posts.author: niftymonk
posts.text: Avis short squeeze. US market is Crazy — The tweet is sarcasm BTW, but what people dont understand in indian markets microcap, smallcap and SME stocks are even more manipulated with the circuit limits.

posts.author: bearishbhaiya
posts.text: Avis short squeeze. US market is Crazy — In general, not specific to Avis. Removing circuits or at least rethinking how they work. I feel, the current system creates an illusion of safety. A stock hitting upper circuits 10 days in a row with no sellers isn’t price discovery. When it reverses, retail is stuck watching lower circuits with no exits. I maybe thinkin brutal, if circuits were gone, the real price would be found in a day or two. Right now you wait across weeks and trap more people on the way down.

posts.author: BB789
posts.text: Avis short squeeze. US market is Crazy — How does efficient and inefficient market theories play here? Here’s a much simpler theory: Money in stocks go brrrr. Money out stocks blows. Whoever has money decides the direction, regardless of world war 3 or nuclear destruction of countries.

posts.author: bearishbhaiya
posts.text: Avis short squeeze. US market is Crazy — Something feels off about the US markets and not many of us even know this happened. Long post ahead. There’s a rental car company in the US called Avis Budget Group. Ticker is $CAR. This company lost nearly $1 billion last year, has $25.8 billion in debt. They are fundamentally terrible but there stock went up 500% last month. I mean image 910×472 30.5 KB Avis has around 35 million total shares. Two hedge funds own 71.5% of it. SRS Investment Management holds 49% and their founder sits on Avis’s board. Pentwater Capital quietly bought up another 22% wh…
